frameworks.name: temporal causal learning
frameworks.one_line: hierarchical generative model infers a singular cause from inter-stimulus interval structure

frameworks.name: prediction error theories
frameworks.one_line: associative learning driven by mismatches between expected and obtained outcomes

frameworks.name: timing theories
frameworks.one_line: learning shaped by the measured passage of time between events

frameworks.name: timescale invariance
frameworks.one_line: single assumption used to derive power-law and scale-invariant phenomena

core_question: Can a single timescale-invariant generative model reconcile timing and prediction error accounts of associative learning?
key_assumptions: timescale invariance of the learning process
a singular cause underlies a rewarding stimulus
inter-stimulus interval structure carries causal information
inference can be implemented by a biologically plausible algorithm
key_findings: model explains power-law dependence of acquisition on inter-trial intervals
model reproduces timescale invariance in response profiles
biologically plausible inference algorithm recapitulates timing and prediction error phenomena
theory predicts neural signals with distinct dynamics for causal associations and temporal structure
interpretations: timing and prediction error accounts can be unified under temporal causal inference
timescale invariance is a sufficient principle for diverse learning phenomena
